listOfQuestions.question: In Music Theory, what does "Pitch" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: How high or low a sound is perceived
wrongAnswers: The regular pulse in music
The combination of notes sounded together
How loud or soft music is played

listOfQuestions.question: In Music Theory, what does "Rhythm" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The pattern of sounds and silences in time
wrongAnswers: The speed of the beat
Three or more notes sounded together
The tone color or quality of a sound

listOfQuestions.question: In Music Theory, what does "Beat" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The regular pulse in music
wrongAnswers: The grouping of beats into repeating patterns
An ordered set of pitches
How notes are started, connected, or separated

listOfQuestions.question: In Music Theory, what does "Tempo" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The speed of the beat
wrongAnswers: A segment of music containing a set number of beats
The tonal center of a piece of music
Smoothly connected notes

listOfQuestions.question: In Music Theory, what does "Meter" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The grouping of beats into repeating patterns
wrongAnswers: A sequence of pitches heard as a musical line
The distance between two pitches
Short separated notes

listOfQuestions.question: In Music Theory, what does "Measure" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A segment of music containing a set number of beats
wrongAnswers: The combination of notes sounded together
How loud or soft music is played
A musical ending or point of rest

listOfQuestions.question: In Music Theory, what does "Melody" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A sequence of pitches heard as a musical line
wrongAnswers: Three or more notes sounded together
The tone color or quality of a sound
A short recurring musical idea

listOfQuestions.question: In Music Theory, what does "Harmony" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The combination of notes sounded together
wrongAnswers: An ordered set of pitches
How notes are started, connected, or separated
Creating music spontaneously while performing

listOfQuestions.question: In Music Theory, what does "Chord" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Three or more notes sounded together
wrongAnswers: The tonal center of a piece of music
Smoothly connected notes
How high or low a sound is perceived

listOfQuestions.question: In Music Theory, what does "Scale" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: An ordered set of pitches
wrongAnswers: The distance between two pitches
Short separated notes
The pattern of sounds and silences in time

listOfQuestions.question: In Music Theory, what does "Key" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The tonal center of a piece of music
wrongAnswers: How loud or soft music is played
A musical ending or point of rest
The regular pulse in music

listOfQuestions.question: In Music Theory, what does "Interval" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The distance between two pitches
wrongAnswers: The tone color or quality of a sound
A short recurring musical idea
The speed of the beat

listOfQuestions.question: In Music Theory, what does "Dynamics" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: How loud or soft music is played
wrongAnswers: How notes are started, connected, or separated
Creating music spontaneously while performing
The grouping of beats into repeating patterns

listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The tone color or quality of a sound
wrongAnswers: Smoothly connected notes
How high or low a sound is perceived
A segment of music containing a set number of beats

listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: How notes are started, connected, or separated
wrongAnswers: Short separated notes
The pattern of sounds and silences in time
A sequence of pitches heard as a musical line

The regular pulse in music
The combination of notes sounded together

The speed of the beat
Three or more notes sounded together

listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A musical ending or point of rest
wrongAnswers: Creating music spontaneously while performing

An ordered set of pitches

listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A short recurring musical idea
